anchor.snippet: It states that a function on the natural numbers can be calculated by an effective method if and only if it is computable by a Turing machine
formalized the definition of the class of general recursive functions
A function on the natural numbers is called λ-computable if the corresponding function on the Church numerals can be represented by a term of the λ-calculus
a function on the natural numbers is called Turing computable if some Turing machine computes the corresponding function on encoded natural numbers
proved that these three formally defined classes of computable functions coincide

anchor.snippet: a method each step of which is precisely predetermined and which is certain to produce the answer in a finite number of steps

anchor.snippet: the words "effectively calculable" will mean "produced by any intuitively 'effective' means whatsoever"

anchor.section: Statement in Church's and Turing's words
anchor.snippet: The thesis can be stated as: Every effectively calculable function is a computable function
proved (1936) that the Entscheidungsproblem is unsolvable: there is no algorithm that can determine whether a well formed formula has a beta normal form

annotations.label: Equivalence of λ-calculus and Turing machines

anchor.snippet: Turing showed that the classes of functions defined by λ-calculus and Turing machines coincided
All three definitions are equivalent, so it does not matter which one is used
Every effectively calculable function (effectively decidable predicate) is general recursive
The following classes of partial functions are coextensive
Turing's thesis that every function which would naturally be regarded as computable is computable under his definition
What can be calculated by a device satisfying principles I–IV is computable
Sieg presents a series of constraints on the behavior of a computor
a function which is computable ['reckonable'] in one of the systems S i , or even in a system of transfinite type, is already computable [reckonable] in S 1

annotations.label: Infinite RE set contains infinite recursive subset

anchor.snippet: Each infinite recursively enumerable (RE) set contains an infinite recursive set
All physically computable functions are Turing-computable

annotations.label: Logarithmic slowdown of universal Turing machine

anchor.snippet: a (multi-tape) universal Turing machine only suffers a logarithmic slowdown factor in simulating any Turing machine
A probabilistic Turing machine can efficiently simulate any realistic model of computation
'Reasonable' machines can simulate each other within a polynomially bounded overhead in time and a constant-factor overhead in space
A quantum Turing machine can efficiently simulate any realistic model of computation
This function takes an input n and returns the largest number of symbols that a Turing machine with n states can print before halting, when run with no input

annotations.label: Busy Beaver and the halting problem

anchor.snippet: Finding an upper bound on the busy beaver function is equivalent to solving the halting problem
